mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive equity returns as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive market repricing over the following week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of post-recession periods, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll increases correlate with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 220 coincide with positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or inflation fails to predict market direction after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and declining unemployment rates confirm a broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and falling UNRATE yields negative returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and slowing output growth, triggering negative market repricing over the following month as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent.
